data.content:
Implementation plan for knots-7048

Context gathered:
- Canonical managed skill sources live under skills/: knots.md, knots_create.md, knots_e2e.md, and knots_plan_orchestrator.md. src/managed_skills_inventory.rs includes those files and renders them for install/update.
- Installed managed copies exist under .agents/skills and .claude/skills, and the currently checked pairs are byte-identical.
- The README already documents lease-backed agent identity and handoff metadata, but the managed skills do not currently include lease or handoff guidance.

Proposed implementation steps:
1. Update skills/knots.md with an identity/lease section before execution: create a lease with kno lease create, claim with kno claim <id> --lease <lease-id>, and use the same lease when advancing. State that notes, handoff capsules, state transitions, and gate decisions take agent identity from the bound lease. Add a warning not to copy deprecated identity flags from old logs or command history because they are ignored.
2. Update skills/knots_create.md so creation guidance starts from a lease: kno lease create, then kno new ... --lease <lease-id>. Add a required handoff step after creation using kno update <id> -H "<capsule>" and explain that note/capsule authorship comes from the bound lease; without the lease authorship may be unknown.
3. Update skills/knots_e2e.md so both the initial e2e claim and every re-claim include --lease <lease-id>, and so the advance examples carry --lease <lease-id>. Include the same lease identity warning and a reminder to attach handoff capsules for step-to-step context when the workflow prompt asks for them.
4. Update skills/knots_plan_orchestrator.md consistently, since it launches/coordinates agents: orchestrator-created or delegated work should allocate or pass a lease per launched agent, ensure workers claim with --lease, and expect handoff capsules/notes to inherit identity from those leases.
5. Refresh installed copies under .agents/skills/*/SKILL.md and .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md from the canonical skills content, either via kno skills update for the relevant tools or by applying the same source text exactly. Keep source and installed copies in sync.
6. Add focused regression assertions in src/managed_skills_tests_ext.rs for rendered skill content: knots-create includes kno new ... --lease and kno update <id> -H; knots and knots-e2e include lease-backed claim/advance guidance; all relevant skills mention lease identity inheritance and warn that deprecated identity flags are ignored. Avoid examples that teach the deprecated flags as usable commands.
7. Add or extend an integration test in tests/cli_skills.rs to install managed skills into a temp repo and assert the installed SKILL.md files contain the lease/handoff text, proving kno skills distribution reflects the new content.

Verification strategy:
- Run grep -ri "lease\|handoff" .agents/skills/knots* .claude/skills/knots* skills/knots* and confirm the expected managed skill files report hits.
- Run the focused managed skill tests, likely cargo test managed_skills --lib and cargo test --test cli_skills knots_e2e_skill_documents_invocation_precedence or the new named install test.
- Run make lint or make sanity before merge, subject to runtime cost and the repo requirement.
- Manually follow the updated knots-create flow in a temp repo: create a lease, create a knot with --lease, add a handoff capsule with -H, then inspect the knot JSON/text to confirm the handoff author is not unknown.

Risks and notes:
- The CLI deprecation help already exists; the main risk is stale installed skill copies if only skills/*.md changes. Treat installed copies and distribution tests as part of the deliverable.
- Keep guidance concise so skill files remain well below the 500-line limit and avoid turning deprecated identity flags into copy-paste examples.
- No child knots appear necessary for this work.
